step_by_step_reasoning: Identify two distinct environments separated by VPC Peering: the AWS Data account and the AWS User Experience & Processing account. The Data account contains an S3 bucket and an EKS cluster. The Processing account features an EKS cluster, an SQS queue, an Auto Scaling Group (ASG) for processing, and an S3 bucket. A User actor interacts with the processing pipeline. We map these to 8 nodes and define two flows: one for data retrieval and one for upload/processing.

graph.name: Hexagon Digital Reality Geospatial Platform
graph.notes: Hexagon's cloud-based platform for storing geospatial data and processing user uploads. It separates data storage from processing using VPC peering for secure, low-latency access, and scales compute-heavy processing using EC2 Auto Scaling Groups.

nodes.name: Users & Devices
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Drag-and-drop web users or connected physical devices uploading geospatial data.

nodes.name: Data EKS
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Handles data streaming back to users from the main data account.

nodes.name: Data S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Single source of truth S3 bucket storing large, high-precision geospatial datasets.

nodes.name: VPC Peering
nodes.notes: Enables secure and private connection across multiple AWS accounts over the AWS backbone.

nodes.name: UX & Processing EKS
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Runs user experience logic and coordinate ingestion queues.

nodes.name: Processing SQS
nodes.notes: Buffers incoming upload notifications and processing jobs.

nodes.name: Upload/Output S3
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Holds direct user uploads and final processed output assets.

nodes.name: EC2 ASG
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Runs pipeline containers on EC2 instances tailored for specific CPU/Memory profiles.

edges.notes: Reads data from central S3 storage.
Delivers data to the user-facing EKS cluster.
Direct file upload using pre-signed S3 URL.
S3 Event Notification triggers queue message.
EKS container listens to SQS queue and coordinates tasks.
Dispatches jobs to pipeline EC2 instances.
Saves final processed outputs back to S3.
